response.justification: While the candidate is a senior-level software engineer with strong communication and adaptability, this role requires deep C++ expertise (5-10 years hands-on on Windows), SQL Server/Oracle proficiency, and capital markets domain experience. The resume shows only 6 months of documented C++ work (at National Instruments), no Windows platform development, no SQL Server/Oracle, and zero financial services exposure. Recent career trajectory (past 5+ years) is in web development (TypeScript, Node.js, React) moving away from systems programming, making this a structural mismatch for a capital markets C++ role.
strengths: Senior-level engineer with 15+ years software development experience and proven ability to master diverse technology stacks
Excellent communication demonstrated through workshops, talks, and contributions to technical communities
Experience with CI/CD pipelines and infrastructure work (useful for capital markets development environments)
concerns: Only 6 months of documented C++ experience versus the mandatory 5-10 years requirement; no evidence of active C++ practice in recent years
No demonstrated Windows platform development experience; career focused on web, Linux, and cloud technologies
Complete absence of financial services, capital markets, investment banking, or fixed-income domain knowledge; no SQL Server or Oracle experience

key_factors.factor: C++ experience gap (6 months vs. 5-10 years required)
No Windows platform or capital markets domain background
Recent career trajectory away from systems programming toward web development
